mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher expected corporate revenues.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increased over the prior month, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A civilian unemployment rate above 6% indicates substantial labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6%, or the mean return during triggered episodes is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in consumer prices combined with a federal funds rate held near zero pushes real policy rates deeply negative, forcing yield-seeking investors into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25 and CPIAUCSL higher than 3 months prior
failure_condition: The joint signal from CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produces a 20-day Rank IC below 0.03, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of joint-signal episodes during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production growth signals weakening goods-sector profitability and inventory accumulation, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days only when labor markets are already tight and the economy lacks spare capacity to absorb the shock.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: INDPRO slowdowns occurring when UNRATE is below 5.5% fail to predict negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, or the signal's information coefficient is not negative and significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales directly captures consumer expenditure momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through immediate revisions in near-term revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth does not co-move with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one million units indicates a contraction in residential investment and related durable goods demand, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days due to downward earnings revisions in construction and banking sectors.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1000, or the average triggered return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate provide a corroborated signal of labor market tightening, which boosts aggregate income expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS change and UNRATE change yields a 60-day Rank IC below 0.03, or the joint signal fails to outperform either single-series signal by at least 50% in hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.5% signals the end of zero-interest-rate policy and the onset of monetary normalization, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns during episodes with FEDFUNDS above 0.5% are not negative in at least 55% of instances, or the mean 20-day return is not below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
